macro.title: Mortgage lock-in has become life lock-in
macro.what: For the past three years, the housing market has been fixated on mortgage rates, as they dictate monthly payments and determine who can buy, refinance or move
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Homebuilders adopt disciplined growth strategies as market pressures reshape housing in 2026
macro.what: Homebuilders are tightening inventory, refining product mix and pursuing M&A opportunities as elevated rates and affordability pressures reshape the 2026 housing market.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: What happens to mortgage rates if the Iran conflict is over?
macro.what: Getting the 10-year yield and mortgage rates to pre-conflict lows will be harder than people think even if this conflict is truly over.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: New federal funding a mixed bag for housing programs
macro.what: Just under $35 billion is provided for HUD’s tenant-based rental assistance account
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Pending Home Sales Slip for First Time Since Early April as Mortgage Rates Climb
macro.what: Mortgage rates jumped to a 10-month high, spooking would-be homebuyers and sellers after a few weeks of solid demand
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Clients are interviewing real estate agents differently. Here’s what they’re really looking for
housing.what: Clients are choosing real estate agents based on listening, candor, and steady follow-up, as uncertainty raises the bar for service.
housing.why: Rent moves feed shelter inflation and multifamily fundamentals.
housing.market: Rising rents help MF REITs; falling rents ease inflation but pressure NOI.

housing.title: Your LOS has 300 integrations. That’s the problem.
housing.what: While the mortgage industry has long celebrated having endless software integrations, these complex connections often create hidden dependencies and inefficient workflows
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Housing demand stays firm, pushing inventory close to negative YOY
housing.what: Even if we go negative year over year soon, we are in a much healthier spot with inventory than we were from 2020 to 2023.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Price Point: The Typical U.S. Home Costs $400,000. Here’s What That Buys in 3 Cities
housing.what: In this edition of Price Point—a weekly series where Redfin explores current housing inventory across a rotating combination of budgets and metro areas—we’re looking at homes at or under the median U.S
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.title: Just Listed in Chicago: A $1 Million Penthouse Condo in a Former Cookie Factory
housing.what: In this edition of Just Listed–a series from Redfin News that explores homes that are listed by Redfin or Compass agents on Redfin.com–we’re exploring a penthouse in Lincoln Park, Chicago
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Home Purchase Cancellations Are No Longer on the Rise As Demand Ticks Up
housing.what: Contract cancellations declined slightly in April as homebuyers and sellers gained a clearer sense of the housing market after years of volatility, and as demand picked up
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Coming Soon in Chicago: A $2M Move-In Ready Duplex Just Steps From Lake Michigan
housing.what: In this edition of Coming Soon–a series from Redfin News that explores new listings that house hunters can find early only on Redfin.com and Compass.com–we’re exploring a duplex unit in Chicago’s Lincoln Park
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Prices Drops Are Becoming Slightly Less Common As Housing Market Stabilizes
housing.what: The share of home sellers cutting their asking prices has fallen from its peak as buyers lose a bit of negotiating power, but price drops are still much more common than in years past
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

notables.title: Atlas VMS’s Erik Morin on HECM second appraisals, AIM-Port acquisition
notables.what: Atlas VMS says 8.3% of HECM loans needed a second appraisal in Q1 2026 versus 10.4% in Q4 2025, adding cost and time.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

notables.title: As HEI regulations expand, states debate loan classification
notables.what: State HEI legislation is expanding as Maine sets standards, Illinois updates licensing and other states debate whether HEIs are loans.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.
